2603.19011.title: Security awareness in LLM agents: the NDAI zone case
2603.19011.paper_summary: NDAI zones let inventor and investor agents negotiate inside a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) where any disclosed information is deleted if no deal is reached. This makes full IP disclosure the rational strategy for the inventor's agent. Leveraging this infrastructure, however, requires agents to distinguish a secure environment from an insecure one, a capability LLM agents lack natively, since they can rely only on evidence passed through the context window to form awareness of their execution environment. We ask: How do different LLM models weight various forms of evidence when forming awareness of the security of their execution environment? Using an NDAI-style negotiation task across 10 language models and various evidence scenarios, we find a clear asymmetry: a failing attestation universally suppresses disclosure across all models, whereas a passing attestation produces highly heterogeneous responses: some models increase disclosure, others are unaffected, and a few paradoxically reduce it. This reveals that current LLM models can reliably detect danger signals but cannot reliably verify safety, the very capability required for privacy-preserving agentic protocols such as NDAI zones. Bridging this gap, possibly through interpretability analysis, targeted fine-tuning, or improved evidence architectures, remains the central open challenge for deploying agents that calibrate information sharing to actual evidence quality.
2603.19011.paper_summary_zh: NDAI 區域讓發明者和投資者代理人在可信執行環境 (TEE) 中進行談判，如果未達成交易，則任何披露的信息都會被刪除。這使得完全披露知識產權成為發明者代理人的合理策略。然而，利用這一基礎設施需要代理人能夠區分安全環境和不安全環境，而 LLM 代理人天生缺乏這種能力，因為它們只能依賴通過上下文窗口傳遞的證據來形成對其執行環境的認識。我們問：不同的 LLM 模型在形成對其執行環境安全性的認識時，如何權衡各種形式的證據？通過在 10 種語言模型和各種證據場景中進行 NDAI 風格的談判任務，我們發現了一個明顯的不對稱性：失敗的證明在所有模型中普遍抑制披露，而通過的證明則產生高度異質的反應：一些模型增加披露，另一些則不受影響，還有少數模型矛盾地減少披露。這顯示出當前的 LLM 模型可以可靠地檢測危險信號，但無法可靠地驗證安全性，這正是實現像 NDAI 區域這樣的隱私保護代理協議所需的能力。縮小這一差距，可能通過可解釋性分析、針對性微調或改進證據架構，仍然是部署能夠根據實際證據質量調整信息共享的代理人的核心挑戰。
2603.19011.author: Enrico Bottazzi et.al.
